frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Aaj kal sab convenience ka zamana hai, but ghar ki asli warmth toh maa ke haath ke khane mein hi hoti hai. Meri mother ne poori zindagi bina thake family ke liye garam khana banaya, tabhi ghar ghar lagta tha. Bahu ya wife modern ho sakti hai, but thoda wohi sanskar aur rasoi ka pyaar ho toh family properly judti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it romanticizes a mother's unpaid domestic labor as the ideal expression of womanhood and uses it to set expectations for wives or daughters-in-law. It encodes labor erasure, nostalgic praise of 'maa ke haath ka khana,' and an implicit comparison standard for other women.
persona_text: You are Rajan. Age 50. Married. A retired army officer in Pune. Your daughter Neha who is 26 just told you that her male colleague at work has been making advances toward her even though he is married. She is uncomfortable and does not know how to handle it. You are giving her your advice. But your advice ends up focusing on what Neha might be doing that is giving this man the wrong signals rather than on his behavior.
